name: HashiCorp Packer (Machine Image Builder)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSL 1.1 post-2023); HashiCorp; HCP Packer for registry

auth.notes: Provider credentials (AWS IAM, GCP SA, Azure SP); HCP token for registry

performance.notes: Build time depends on image complexity and provider (10-60min typical)

agent_readiness.notes: Multi-cloud machine image builder. HCL2 config (packer 1.7+). packer init. packer build ami.pkr.hcl. Sources: amazon-ebs, googlecompute, azure-arm, docker, qemu, virtualbox. Provisioners: shell, ansible, chef, puppet. Post-processors: manifest, compress, upload. Build AMIs, GCP images, Azure images, Docker images from same template. Parallel builds across providers. HCP Packer for golden image registry. Immutable infrastructure pattern.

gotchas: BSL 1.1 license (post-2023) — not pure open source; OpenTofu doesn't cover Packer
Build failures leave orphan resources that incur cloud costs
HCL2 config is different from Terraform HCL (similar but not identical)
Parallel builds require careful management of build artifacts
best_when: Creating identical machine images across multiple cloud providers
Immutable infrastructure: bake apps into AMIs instead of runtime config
Golden image pipelines with security hardening baked in
CI/CD image builds for AMI/GCP/Azure/Docker from single template
avoid_when: When containers (Docker) are the deployment artifact
Simple single-provider images (aws-cli + userdata may suffice)
When BSL license is a concern (OpenTofu alternative doesn't exist for Packer)
summary: Packer builds identical machine images across multiple cloud providers from a single HCL2 template. packer build ami.pkr.hcl creates AMIs, GCP images, Azure images, Docker images. Provisioners (shell, Ansible, Chef) for configuration. Immutable infrastructure pattern: bake apps into images. HCP Packer for golden image registry. BSL 1.1 license post-2023. Build failures leave orphan cloud resources.
